title: plugin-manager:install-plugin-use-case
description: Use case that orchestrates plugin installation. Loads the plugin, calls its install method, and returns a generic result.
context: Use case that orchestrates plugin installation. Loads the plugin, calls its install method, and returns a generic result.

rules:
```typescript
interface InstallPluginInput {
  readonly pluginName: string // npm package name
  readonly projectRoot: string
  readonly options?: Record<string, unknown> // plugin-specific options
}
```

```typescript
interface InstallPluginOutput {
  readonly success: boolean
  readonly message: string // human-readable result
  readonly data?: unknown // plugin-specific result
}
```

The use case MUST:

1. Load the plugin via `PluginLoader`
2. Call the plugin's `install()` method with project root and options
3. Return a generic result (no config mutation — that is the CLI's responsibility)
On failure, the use case MUST throw an appropriate error (`PluginNotFoundError` or `PluginValidationError`).

constraints: This use case does NOT modify config — it only calls the plugin's install.
Config mutation is the CLI's responsibility via ConfigWriter.

scenarios.name: Successful install
when: InstallPlugin is executed with valid plugin

scenarios.name: Plugin not found
when: InstallPlugin is executed with non-existent plugin
